title: Public Methods Should Only Throw One Checked Exception
description: Public methods should not throw more than one kind of checked exception. This is to keep the complexity for callers reasonable. Therefore, using checked exceptions forces method callers to manage errors, either by propagating them or by handling them. This rule makes exceptions a part of the method's API.
recommendation: To make this code compliant, consider throwing only one type of checked exception in your public methods. For instance, instead of throwing multiple exceptions such as IOException and SQLException, throw a single application-level exception.
impact: Violating this rule can increase the complexity of your code and make it harder to maintain and debug. It may also lead to unhandled exceptions which can crash your application.

checks.message: To make this code compliant, consider throwing only one type of checked exception in your public methods.
